findings.summary: Component count (62), net count (82), and BOM groupings are all accurate; UART detection correctly identifies CH340C (U2) to ESP32 (U4) connection on RXD/TXD nets; USB Type-C compliance checks are ...

correct.description: The analyzer reports 62 total components with correct type breakdowns (19 resistors, 12 capacitors, 8 diodes, 6 LEDs, 5 ICs, 4 relays, 4 connectors, 2 transistors, 2 switches). All 62 component entries in the components list match the schematic. The 82-net count matches the actual schematic. BOM groupings by value/footprint are correct, including the 0E resistors (R1, R9, R12), the 10K pull-ups, the 2K2 LED/relay resistors, and the separate 10k (lowercase) vs 10K (uppercase) groupings.

The analyzer correctly identifies two UART nets: RXD (U2 TXD-output pin 2 to U4 RXD0/IO3 pin 34) and TXD (U2 RXD-input pin 3 to U4 TXD0/IO1 pin 35). Both are shown with pin_count=2. The cross_domain_signals section correctly identifies these as crossing between the +3.3V (U2) and ESP_3V3 (U4) domains with needs_level_shifter=false, which is correct since both are nominally 3.3V domains.

J1 is a USB_C_Receptacle_USB2.0_16P. The analyzer correctly finds R2 and R3 (both 5.1K) on CC1 and CC2 respectively for downstream-facing port compliance. VBUS ESD protection passes (D1 LESD5D5.0CT1G on VBUS-GND). VBUS decoupling fails correctly — no bulk capacitor is present directly on the VBUS net. The dp_series_resistor and dm_series_resistor are correctly marked 'info' (D+ and D- connect directly from USB connector to CH340C without explicit series resistors). The usb_esd_ic pass for D2/D4 TVS diodes on D+/D- is correct.

The SDA net connects U4 pin 33 (IO21) to J4 pin 2, and SCL connects U4 pin 36 (IO22) to J4 pin 3. The analyzer correctly identifies both as I2C lines with has_pull_up=false and pullup_resistor=None. This is an accurate observation — no pull-up resistors are present on these nets in the schematic, which is a genuine design concern for I2C operation.

The design_observations entry {category: 'decoupling', component: 'U2', value: 'CH340C', rails_without_caps: ['+3.3V'], rails_with_caps: []} is accurate. The +3.3V net has only U2 VCC and a power symbol — no decoupling capacitor is placed on this net. The AMS1117 output decoupling caps C7 and C8 are on the separate +3V3 net, not on +3.3V. This is both a real design issue and a correctly detected observation.

The power_regulators entry correctly identifies U3 as an LDO with topology='LDO', output_rail='+3V3', estimated_vout=3.3, vref_source='fixed_suffix'. The regulator_caps observation correctly flags missing_caps={input: '__unnamed_13'} — the AMS1117 input net (__unnamed_13, bridged from +5V via R9 0E) has C5 (10uF) as its only capacitor but the analyzer considers the named rail incomplete. Output side has C7 (10uF) and C8 (0.1uF) on the +3V3 net, which is correctly noted as having bulk and bypass coverage.

RELAY_1 net connects K1 coil pin 2, D6 cathode, D10 anode, and U1 pin 13 (O4). RELAY_2 connects K2 coil pin 2, D7 cathode, D11 anode, and U1 pin 12 (O5). RELAY_3 connects K3 coil pin 2, D8 cathode, D12 anode, and U1 pin 11 (O6). RELAY_4 connects K4 coil pin 2, D13 anode, and U1 pin 10 (O7). All these pin_type assignments (open_collector for ULN2003A outputs, passive for relay/diode pins) are correct. The RELAY_5V net correctly groups all four relay coil pin 1 connections plus the ULN2003A COM pin (pin 9) and the LED anode supply resistors.

incorrect.description: Three 0-ohm resistors create power rail bridges that the analyzer does not recognise as domain connections: (1) R12 (0E) bridges +3V3 (AMS1117 VO output) to ESP_3V3 (ESP32 VDD), making them the same physical rail. (2) R9 (0E) bridges +5V to the unnamed net that feeds AMS1117 VI input. (3) R1 (0E) bridges +5V to RELAY_5V (relay coil supply). As a result, the power_domains section shows +3V3 and ESP_3V3 as independent domains with no supply path connection, which is incorrect. The cross_domain_signals list reports RXD/TXD as crossing between +3.3V and ESP_3V3, flagging needs_level_shifter=false — while the logic-level answer is correct, the domain separation itself misrepresents the topology since ESP_3V3 is simply +3V3 passed through a 0-ohm jumper.

The +3.3V net in the schematic contains only U2 VCC (power_in) and a #PWR010 power symbol (power_in). There is no power_out pin and no PWR_FLAG on this net. The AMS1117 output is on the distinct net +3V3, and R12 bridges +3V3 to ESP_3V3 — but nothing bridges +3V3 or any other supply to +3.3V. The pwr_flag_warnings correctly identifies this as "Power rail '+3.3V' has power_in pins but no power_out or PWR_FLAG", but the erc_warnings section does NOT include a no_driver warning for +3.3V (it only covers ESP_3V3, EN, P, N). This is an omission — the ERC no_driver logic should also flag +3.3V. This is likely a schematic design error where the designer intended +3.3V and +3V3 to be the same rail but used different power symbol names.

Q1 and Q2 are SS8050 NPN transistors used in the standard ESP32 auto-reset/auto-flash circuit. Q1: B connected through R10 to the DTR net (driven by U2 CH340C pin 13 ~{DTR}), E on RTS, C on EN (ESP32 enable). Q2: B connected through R11 to the RTS net (driven by U2 CH340C pin 14 ~{RTS}), E on DTR, C on IO0. The analyzer correctly identifies the BJT type, base/collector/emitter nets, and base resistors. However, base_driver_ics=[] for both transistors — the analyzer does not follow the net from the base resistor back through the intermediate unnamed net to the CH340C DTR/RTS outputs. This means the auto-reset circuit is detected as transistors with an unknown driver rather than being explicitly linked to U2.

D3 has its anode (pin A) on VBUS and cathode (pin K) on +5V. This configuration allows USB VBUS to supply the +5V rail when no AC power is present (the HLK-PM01 AC-DC converter is the primary +5V source). This is a power-ORing or auctioneering diode topology, not reverse-polarity protection. Reverse-polarity protection would place a diode in series with the input to block a reversed supply. The analyzer classifies D3 as type='reverse_polarity' with protected_net='+5V' and clamp_net='VBUS', which is functionally and semantically incorrect.

missed.description: U1 (ULN2003A) drives relay coils K1–K4 via its open-collector outputs (O4/O7 on RELAY_1–RELAY_4 nets), with snubber diodes D10–D13 (SS34 Schottky) across each coil. Each relay output also has an LED indicator (D6–D9) with a 2K2 series resistor (R5–R8) connected from RELAY_5V to the relay coil node. The ESP32 (U4) drives U1 inputs via SIG_1/IO25 through SIG_4/IO18 nets. The analyzer does not produce a structured relay-driver observation — no 'relay_driver' category appears in design_observations, and the subcircuit for U1 lists neighbors correctly but does not summarise the functional pattern (Darlington array driving relay coils with flyback diodes and coil-parallel LED indicators).

findings.summary: Component extraction: 62 components, correct type classifications; AMS1117-3.3 LDO regulator detected with correct output voltage 3.3V and output rail +3V3; I2C bus missing pull-ups correctly detec...

correct.description: All 62 components correctly parsed with right types: 19 resistors, 12 caps, 8 diodes, 6 LEDs, 4 relays, 5 ICs (including HLK-PM01, CH340C, AMS1117-3.3, ULN2003A, ESP32-WROOM-32), 2 transistors, 4 connectors, 2 switches.
Regulator correctly identified as LDO, output_rail='+3V3', estimated_vout=3.3, vref_source='fixed_suffix'. Decoupling caps C7 (10uF) and C8 (0.1uF) on output rail correctly found.
Both SDA and SCL nets show has_pull_up: false with no pull_ups listed. Confirmed in source: these nets only connect to U4 (ESP32) and J4 (I2C header), no pull-up resistors present.
cc1_pulldown_5k1: pass, cc2_pulldown_5k1: pass. Confirmed: R2 and R3 are 5.1K on CC1/CC2 lines. vbus_decoupling: fail is also correct since VBUS has no dedicated decoupling cap.

incorrect.description: differential_pairs shows esd_protection: ['U2'] — but CH340C (U2) is the USB-UART IC itself, not an ESD protection device. The actual ESD protection is D2 (LESD5D5.0CT1G TVS on D+) and D4 (LESD5D5.0CT1G TVS on D-), which are TVS diodes. has_esd: true is correct but the attribution list is wrong.

power_budget.+3V3.ic_count=0, estimated_load_mA=0, but U4 draws power from ESP_3V3 which is fed by U3's output (+3V3) through R12 (0-ohm jumper). The analyzer fails to trace through the global label ESP_3V3 to attribute U4's power consumption to the +3V3 rail. This significantly understates the load on U3.

This is actually correct: the +3.3V net (power symbol, separate from +3V3) has no decoupling caps. The net is fed by U4's VCC output indirectly but has no local cap. However, the power_rails list shows '+3.3V' but decoupling_coverage only covers '+5V' and '+3V3', leaving +3.3V with no coverage summary. The missing decoupling cap on CH340C's VCC is a real design concern.

ESP_3V3 is a global label used as a net name (connects U3 output via R12 to U4 VDD and pull-up resistors). The ERC fires 'no_driver' because it sees only passive/input pins and no power_out driver on that net. In practice, R12 (0-ohm) bridges +3V3 to ESP_3V3, so there IS a driver. The analyzer doesn't follow zero-ohm jumpers when evaluating power drivers.

missed.description: The design uses a ULN2003A (Darlington array) driving 4 HF46F relay coils via RELAY_1–RELAY_4 nets, with flyback diodes (SS34 Schottky D3/D10/D11/D12/D13). No relay_driver signal path or Darlington array detection is reported in the signal_path_detectors or design_analysis sections.

The design has AC Line ('P' net) and AC Neutral ('N' net) connected to PS1 (HLK-PM01 AC/DC) and relay switch contacts (K1-K4 pins 3/4) on J2. This is mains voltage (240V/120V AC) on a PCB with 3.3V logic. No high-voltage safety warning is generated in erc_warnings, design_analysis, or any other section.
